title: MachineFromFileInput Object
description: Object used as input to create a machine from a local file. It is used by the Machine.create method. The object holds the data needed to create a machine from a local machine file.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: create
methods.description: Creates a MachineFromFileInput object to be used as input for Machine.create method.

properties.name: filePath
properties.description: The path to a file to act as a base for creating a machine from. The filePath is expected to be an absolute path to the local machine file.

properties.name: ignoreSimulationModel
properties.description: Whether or not to ignore the simulation model when creating/loading the machine. If set to true the simulation model will not be set on the new machine.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

classType.title: MachineFromFileInput.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

create.title: MachineFromFileInput.create Method
create.description: Creates a MachineFromFileInput object to be used as input for Machine.create method.
create.return_description: The newly created "MachineFromFileInput" object in a valid state.

parameters.name: filePath
parameters.description: The path to a machine file to be processed. The filePath is expected to be an absolute path to the machine file on disk.

filePath.title: MachineFromFileInput.filePath Property
filePath.description: The path to a file to act as a base for creating a machine from. The filePath is expected to be an absolute path to the local machine file.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = machineFromFileInput_var.filePath

ignoreSimulationModel.title: MachineFromFileInput.ignoreSimulationModel Property
ignoreSimulationModel.description: Whether or not to ignore the simulation model when creating/loading the machine. If set to true the simulation model will not be set on the new machine. Remarks

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = machineFromFileInput_var.ignoreSimulationModel

# Set the value of the property.
machineFromFileInput_var.ignoreSimulationModel = propertyValue

isValid.title: MachineFromFileInput.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = machineFromFileInput_var.isValid

objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():
